frame_analyses.description: Suture block with yellow clamp visible at top. Blue 2-0 silk suture fully visible, coiled on table. Penrose drain target marks visible in background text overlay.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup complete. Suture has not yet been introduced through the Penrose drain.

frame_analyses.description: Blue suture being held by hand. Needle (presumed on one end) is being controlled. Suture appears to be transitioning toward the Penrose drain targets.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hands-on manipulation begins. No instruments visible yet—appears to be manual/non-FLS trainer context.

frame_analyses.description: Blue suture positioned around orange foam model (simulating Penrose drain). Hands are manipulating suture to pass through intended target areas. Suture loop forming around the foam.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture being positioned extracorporeally, not yet passed through formal trainer marks. First target pass appears to be in progress.

frame_analyses.description: Blue suture wrapped around orange foam model. Both hands visible manipulating suture strands. Suture appears to have looped around target region.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture passage around foam proceeding. No visible needle driver or formal instrument use—consistent with hands-on demonstration rather than true FLS trainer box task.

frame_analyses.description: Two distinct loops visible in blue suture around orange foam. Knot formation beginning. Hands manipulating suture to create first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition from suture positioning to knot formation. First throw configuration becoming visible.

frame_analyses.description: Multiple loops of blue suture visible. Hands positioning suture to form throws. Knot structure emerging with three distinct loop regions visible around foam.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Apparent progression through throw sequence. Knot architecture shows multiple turns consistent with extracorporeal knot formation.

frame_analyses.description: Blue suture configured with multiple throws formed. Hands visible manipulating and cinching knot around foam model.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: All three throws appear to be formed and partially tensioned. Knot structure well-defined.

frame_analyses.description: Knot fully cinched around orange foam. Suture tails visible extending from knot. Foam model compressed by knot tension, consistent with slit closure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure and under tension. No visible gap or slack in knot. Slit closure appears complete.

frame_analyses.description: Final knot configuration. Suture tightly cinched. Orange foam showing indentation/closure from knot pressure. Both suture tails visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot cinching complete. No visible slippage or loosening. Slit closure appears maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Hands holding suture with knot fully secured on foam model. One tail visible being held for cutting. Yellow clamp (knot pusher simulator) visible at top of frame.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ready for suture tail cutting phase. Knot remains secure throughout manipulation.

frame_analyses.description: Suture tail being held and positioned for cutting. Yellow clamp at top still visible. Knot remains cinched on foam model.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture cutting phase in progress. Positioning tail for clean separation.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture tails cut. Knot final configuration visible on orange foam model. Suture ends separated and removed from field. Task completion evident.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final state: secure knot on foam model with tails removed. Slit closure maintained. No drain avulsion.

penalties.description: Video demonstrates hands-on suture knot tying on foam model, NOT using FLS trainer box with formal needle drivers, knot pusher tool, or Penrose drain mounted in suture block. This is an instructional/demonstration context, not a formal FLS task assessment.
penalties.note: No penalty applied to score because video is classified as instructional, not a performance evaluation.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw configuration visible in frames 5–6. Appears well-formed and under tension.
Second throw visible in frames 6–7. Part of multi-throw knot structure. Appears to hold tension.
Third throw incorporated into final knot by frame 8. Overall knot structure stable and secure.

suture_placement.note: No formal marked target points visible on foam model. Suture passed around foam but exact deviation unmeasurable without precise anatomical markers.

score_components.note: Score computed for reference only. Video is instructional/demonstration context, NOT a formal FLS trainee performance evaluation.

confidence_rationale: Video is classified as INSTRUCTIONAL demonstration, not a formal FLS task performance. The content shows hands-on foam model knot tying without FLS trainer equipment (no needle driver instruments, no suture block apparatus, no knot pusher tool). Timing, penalty assessment, and technique analysis are provided for educational reference, but scoring confidence is low because this does not represent a genuine FLS task execution in the official trainer box. Frame clarity is adequate, knot formation is visible and appears secure, and slit closure is complete, but the methodology deviation disqualifies this as a scoreable performance.
cannot_determine: Exact suture deviation from marked targets (no visible formal target marks on foam model)
Hand-switching pattern between throws (hands not clearly labeled as dominant/non-dominant)
Instrument type and proper needle driver technique (manual/hands-on approach, not instrument-based FLS methodology)
Knot pusher tool usage (not visible; knots appear hand-cinched)
Whether completion occurred inside official FLS trainer box (appears to be tabletop demonstration)
video_context: This video depicts a hands-on demonstration of extracorporeal knot tying around a foam model, used likely for educational or instructional purposes. It is NOT a formal FLS task assessment in the official trainer apparatus and should not be treated as a scoreable performance for certification or competency validation.
technique_summary: Skilled hand-tied knot formation with three throws progressively built and cinched around foam model. All throws appear secure, no slippage observed, slit closure maintained. However, the instructional format (hands-on foam model vs. FLS trainer box) and absence of formal instrument technique mean this is an educational demonstration rather than a formal FLS performance.
strengths: Secure final cinch with no visible slippage
Clean tail cutting and task completion

demonstrated_techniques: Progressive throw formation with multiple wraps
Hand manipulation and crossing for knot geometry
common_errors_addressed: Video implicitly demonstrates proper knot tightening to avoid slippage
Shows secure cinching practice without over-tensioning foam model
teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: intermediate to expert (clean knot formation, secure cinching, no slippage)

improvement_suggestions: If transitioning to formal FLS task: use official trainer box, needle drivers, and knot pusher tool
Formalize target mark placement on Penrose drain for precise suture deviation measurement
Practice hand-switching protocol if three single throws are required with alternating hands
Reduce suture introduction and tail-cutting phases to under 8s each for expert-level performance
